checkedInputs: current six-agent adversarial compact state review
reviewPhases.disposition: Scope is intentionally all phases because the verified problem spans source hooks, installed settings, Beads boundary, and rollback proof.
Existing ledgers and workflow-health are reused; canonical state is introduced as a compatibility layer with JSONL truth before optional adapters.
Plan requires recorded and observed installed state to be reported separately so live drift cannot look green.

checkedInputs: current Beads and Dolt adversarial review
reviewPhases.disposition: Raw Beads authority and hook-hot-path Dolt use are rejected; Beads is backlog-only and Dolt is optional projection only.
Plan follows the supported lifecycle: PreCompact save, PostCompact record, synchronous SessionStart compact rehydrate.
Plan reuses existing CLIs and tests as facades and projections instead of replacing them wholesale.
Plan avoids hook-triggered compact, raw transcript replay, Beads as active ledger, and default Dolt dependency in lifecycle hooks.

sources.refreshCommand: node scripts/research-competitor-intel.mjs validate-manifest --manifest docs/research/top10-lock.json && node scripts/research-competitor-intel.mjs validate-evidence --evidence docs/research/capability-evidence.json

skillMatrix.trigger: User invoked etrnl-plan for implementation planning.
skillMatrix.evidence: Plan file and deep-stack artifact were created under docs/plans.

skillMatrix.trigger: Plan changes PreCompact, PostCompact, SessionStart, Stop, SessionEnd, and installed hook settings.
skillMatrix.evidence: Hook lifecycle constraints are represented in task groups D, E, and G.

skillMatrix.trigger: New state layer and compatibility adapters risk accidental complexity.
skillMatrix.evidence: Skill routing requires simplifier before final completion.

skillMatrix.trigger: Execution ledger, context-state, workflow-health, hook state, and tool-effectiveness duplicate state concepts.
skillMatrix.evidence: Skill routing requires this pass for migration and projection slices when available.

skillMatrix.trigger: Control-plane health and failure-mode surfacing are touched.
skillMatrix.evidence: Skill routing marks Brooks as useful after implementation if installed.

skillMatrix.trigger: No tenant, money, auth, i18n, Prisma, permissions, or soft-delete domain policy is changed.
skillMatrix.evidence: Plan remains in control-plane hook and workflow infrastructure.

skillMatrix.trigger: This plan touches JavaScript and shell infrastructure, not TypeScript public contracts.
skillMatrix.evidence: Ordinary syntax and workflow checks remain required.
skillMatrix.notApplicableRationale: No exported TypeScript types, API contracts, runtime validation schemas, state machines, branded IDs, reusable type utilities, or cross-layer DTO boundaries are planned.

decisions.decision: Create a narrow canonical state CLI because existing ledgers, context saves, hook tmp state, and effectiveness events are separate islands.
decisions.newSurfaceJustification: A compatibility facade cannot provide one durable compact handoff and stale-verification view without a shared state contract.

decisions.decision: Create shared core logic to keep hooks and CLIs from duplicating schema, validation, privacy, locks, and materialized views.
decisions.newSurfaceJustification: Putting this inside one existing CLI would force unrelated scripts to depend on execution-ledger or workflow-health internals.

decisions.decision: Add an ADR because this changes state authority and Beads/Dolt boundaries.
decisions.newSurfaceJustification: The decision needs durable public explanation separate from a dated implementation plan.

findings.summary: Manual compact reminder is not context-aware and wastes tokens.
findings.evidence: Plan removes compact reminder from repo-owned installed path and adds settings audit coverage.
findings.resolution: Covered by Group D and Group G.

findings.summary: Installed compact restore is async even though compact rehydration must be immediate.
findings.evidence: Plan requires synchronous SessionStart compact restore and install test coverage.
findings.resolution: Covered by Group D and Group G.

findings.summary: Beads raw task authority conflicts with ETRNL execution authority.
findings.evidence: Plan rejects raw Beads startup injection and keeps Beads backlog-only.

findings.summary: Tool-effectiveness live data is empty and cannot yet drive state decisions.
findings.evidence: Plan treats effectiveness as a projection and fixes persistence separately.
findings.resolution: Covered by Groups C and H.

completionAudit.evidence: Plan covers compact lifecycle, state substrate, Beads boundary, Dolt boundary, install/update drift, rollback, and verification gates.
Plan includes both anti-Beads-runtime and Dolt-as-optional-projection positions, resolving them with hook-hot-path constraints.
Plan explicitly includes staged install, update-check, settings-audit, rollback, and live install approval.

tddEvidence.rationaleWhenNotTestFirst: This is a planning artifact; implementation must add the red fixture commands listed in the Test-first execution plan before source code is written.
Plan names the exact failing compact and SessionStart fixtures; red-green evidence is required during implementation.
Plan names the exact async SessionStart and mode-mismatch fixtures; red-green evidence is required during implementation.

completionReconciliation.requestedOutcome: Research hooks system, context, auto compact, and post compact behavior.
completionReconciliation.evidence: Plan evidence cites Claude hook docs, current source hooks, installed settings, and compact lifecycle task groups.

completionReconciliation.requestedOutcome: Explain why Beads, why Dolt, and why not.
completionReconciliation.evidence: Plan keeps Beads backlog-only and Dolt optional projection-only with server-mode/custom-table constraints.

completionReconciliation.requestedOutcome: Decide what to keep and lose from ETRNL.
completionReconciliation.evidence: Plan keeps ledgers, workflow-health, context-state intent, strict hooks, and install gates while dropping noisy compact reminders and tmp-only canonical state.

completionReconciliation.requestedOutcome: Use adversarial review and orthogonal expert findings.
completionReconciliation.evidence: Artifact review phases include CEO, engineering, DX, adversarial, specialist, reuse, and simplifier dispositions.
Plan includes file map, task groups, slices, phases, routing, test-first plan, failure modes, rollback, and verification gates.

reuseBindings.decision: Create ETRNL state core and keep existing CLIs as compatibility surfaces.
reuseBindings.newSurfaceJustification: No existing single surface owns compact handoff, stale verification, typed context entries, tool signals, and Beads links.

reuseBindings.decision: Rewrite existing hooks instead of adding a parallel compact hook family.
Extend existing install and audit surfaces rather than creating a separate installed-state checker.
Create explicit Beads bridge command under ETRNL state instead of using raw bd prime or active execution mirroring.
reuseBindings.newSurfaceJustification: The bridge needs deterministic dry-run, metadata markers, and duplicate-truth checks that Beads issue commands do not provide.

sourceGate.evidence: Research manifest and evidence validators passed; artifact and plan readiness gates are part of finalization.

stagedInstall.evidence: This plan does not mutate installed homes; staged install is required during implementation before live rollout.

stagedDoctor.evidence: Staged doctor is required during implementation after staged install.

rollbackVerification.evidence: Rollback rehearsal is required during implementation before live install.

liveInstallDecision.evidence: Live install requires explicit Victor approval after staged evidence is clean.

postUpgradeCanary.evidence: Post-upgrade canary is required after live install, not during planning.

riskTier.reason: Tier 2 for planning and source implementation readiness; implementation escalates to Tier 3 before live installed-home mutation because compact hooks affect active Claude sessions.
acceptedRisks: Real Claude auto-compact cannot be forced deterministically from source tests; implementation must use lifecycle fixtures plus manual compact smoke before live install.

typescript.ordinaryVerification: node --check for modified JavaScript files; shell syntax checks for modified hook scripts; project TypeScript check remains not applicable unless implementation touches TypeScript.

advancedTypes.notApplicableRationale: No TypeScript public contract or advanced type surface is planned.
